title: Issue Triage Priority Queue
description: Build a function that ranks open-source issues by labels, recency, and comment count. Bugs with failing tests should rank above documentation tasks, stale issues should move down unless they are tagged as good-first-issue, and ties should keep original order.

examples.input: issues=[bug failing-tests, docs stale, good-first-issue stale]

constraints: Each issue has labels, created_at, and comments
hints: Convert labels to numeric weights first.
Use a stable sort so ties preserve input order.
solution_approach: Assign weighted scores for labels and penalties, then stable sort by score descending.

title: Mentor Slot Matcher
description: Given contributor time windows and mentor office-hour windows, return the earliest valid mentor slot for each contributor. A contributor can attend a slot only if the overlap is at least 20 minutes.
constraints: Times are minutes from week start
hints: Sort both lists by start time.
Advance the pointer whose interval ends first.
solution_approach: Use a two-pointer interval overlap scan after sorting windows.
time_complexity: O(n log n + m log m)

title: Patch Dependency Order
description: A repository has patches where some patches depend on others. Return an order to review them so every dependency is reviewed before its dependent patch. If there is a cycle, return an empty list.
hints: This is a topological ordering problem.
Track indegrees and start with zero-indegree patches.
solution_approach: Run Kahn's algorithm over the dependency graph.

title: Changelog Section Builder
description: Group merged pull requests into changelog sections using their labels. Unknown labels should go into Other. Preserve pull-request order inside each section.

examples.input: prs=[feat A, fix B, docs C]

constraints: Each PR has title and labels
hints: Define label-to-section precedence.
Append to arrays instead of repeatedly concatenating strings.
solution_approach: Map labels to buckets and append each PR title to the first matching bucket.

title: Maintainer Load Balancer
description: Assign incoming review requests to maintainers. Each maintainer has supported areas and a current load. Choose the eligible maintainer with the lowest load, breaking ties lexicographically by name, and update their load.
hints: Keep one min-heap per area.
Push the maintainer back after increasing load.
solution_approach: Build heaps keyed by load and name for each area.

title: Contributor Streak Analyzer
description: Given commit dates for contributors, return the longest streak of consecutive active days for each contributor. Multiple commits on the same date count as one active day.
hints: Deduplicate dates per contributor.
Scan sorted days and count consecutive runs.
solution_approach: Group by contributor, sort unique dates, and compute longest consecutive run.

title: Idempotent Payment Retry
description: Design an endpoint handler that processes payment requests with an idempotency key. Repeated requests with the same key must return the original result without charging twice, even if the first attempt succeeded but the client timed out.
constraints: The idempotency key is unique per logical payment
hints: Store request state before calling the external payment provider.
Use a transaction or uniqueness constraint around the key.
solution_approach: Persist a pending record keyed by idempotency key, lock/read it on retries, and return stored results.

title: Rate Limit Sliding Window
description: Implement a per-user rate limiter allowing at most k requests in any rolling window of w seconds. Return whether each incoming request should be allowed.
hints: Use a queue of timestamps per user.
Remove entries older than current_time - window.
solution_approach: Maintain deque windows per user and check size before appending.

title: Webhook Delivery Deduper
description: Given webhook deliveries with event_id and attempt number, process each event exactly once. Return the event_ids that should be processed in arrival order.
hints: Event identity is more important than attempt number.
A set is enough for the basic version.
solution_approach: Track processed event IDs and skip duplicates.

title: Job Queue Visibility Timeout
description: Simulate a queue where popped jobs become invisible for a timeout. If not acknowledged before timeout, they become available again. Support enqueue, pop(time), ack(id), and tick(time).

examples.input: enqueue A, pop@0, pop@5 timeout=10, pop@11

hints: Separate available jobs from invisible jobs.
A min-heap by visibility deadline helps restore expired jobs.
solution_approach: Use a FIFO queue for available jobs and a min-heap for in-flight deadlines.

title: API Version Negotiator
description: Given client-supported API versions and server-supported versions, choose the highest shared version. If none exists, return unsupported.
hints: A set makes membership checks simple.
Scan the smaller or sorted list depending on output requirements.
solution_approach: Build a set from one list and track the maximum common version.

title: Audit Log Compactor
description: Compact an audit log by merging consecutive identical state changes by the same actor on the same entity. Keep the first timestamp and latest final value.

examples.input: A user1 doc1 open, A user1 doc1 open, B user2 doc1 close

hints: Compare each record to the last output record.
Do not merge across a different actor or entity.
solution_approach: Single pass with the output stack as the merge target.

title: Keyboard Command Palette
description: Implement command palette filtering. Given commands, a search query, and disabled command IDs, return visible commands ordered by fuzzy match score, excluding disabled commands.
constraints: Queries contain lowercase or uppercase letters and spaces
hints: Normalize case first.
A subsequence match is a good simple fuzzy match.
solution_approach: Score commands by ordered subsequence positions and sort by score.

title: Virtual List Window
description: Given item height, viewport height, scroll offset, and overscan, return the first and last item indexes that should be rendered in a virtualized list.
hints: Compute visible start and end by division.
Apply overscan and clamp to bounds.
solution_approach: Use arithmetic to map scroll offset to index range.

title: Form Dirty State Tracker
description: Given initial form values and a stream of field updates, return after each update whether the form is dirty. A form is dirty when at least one current field differs from the initial value.
hints: Track only fields that differ.
Remove a field from the dirty set when it returns to its original value.
solution_approach: Maintain current values and a set of dirty field names.

title: Responsive Grid Packer
description: Given card widths and container width, pack cards into rows preserving order. Return row groupings where each row's total width plus gaps does not exceed the container width.
hints: This is first-fit in original order.
Remember gaps only between cards.
solution_approach: Greedily append cards to current row until the next one would overflow.

title: Undo Redo Text Buffer
description: Implement a text buffer that supports type(text), undo(), and redo(). Typing after undo should discard the redo stack. Return the visible text after each operation.

examples.input: type a, type b, undo, type c

hints: Use two stacks of snapshots or patches.
Clear redo on a new edit.
solution_approach: Maintain current text plus undo and redo stacks of prior states.

title: Accessible Toast Queue
description: Given toast events with priority and duration, simulate the visible toast queue where at most three toasts are visible. Higher priority toasts appear before lower priority but older same-priority toasts stay first.

examples.input: add A p1, add B p2

hints: Stable ordering matters inside each priority.
You can sort the active list after inserts for the simple version.
solution_approach: Keep active toasts sorted by priority descending and insertion order ascending.

title: Optimistic Like Reconciler
description: A client applies optimistic like/unlike actions before the server confirms them. Given local actions and server acknowledgements, return the final like count and whether the current user likes the item.
hints: Keep pending operations separate from confirmed state.
Rollback failed optimistic operations.
solution_approach: Track confirmed state plus pending deltas and reconcile each acknowledgement.

title: Cursor Pagination Merger
description: Merge pages from a cursor-paginated API into a client cache. Duplicate IDs may appear across page boundaries and should appear only once in final order.
hints: Use a set of seen IDs.
Append only new records.
solution_approach: Single pass over pages while tracking seen item IDs.

title: Draft Autosave Conflict Resolver
description: A user edits a draft while the server also has a newer version. Given local patches, server patches, and base version, merge non-overlapping field edits and report conflicts for fields changed on both sides.
hints: Compare each patch against the common base field.
Conflict is field-level in this problem.
solution_approach: Build changed-field maps for local and server, merge disjoint keys, report intersecting keys.

title: Feature Flag Evaluator
description: Evaluate feature flags for a user. Rules may target country, plan, percentage rollout, or explicit user IDs. Return enabled flags for the user.
hints: Evaluate explicit user rules before broad rollout rules.
Percentage rollout needs deterministic hashing.
solution_approach: Check rule precedence and use a stable hash for percentage rules.

title: Offline Action Replay
description: A client records actions while offline. When reconnecting, replay actions in order, but drop actions that target entities deleted by earlier replayed actions.

examples.input: update A, delete A, update A

hints: Track deleted entity IDs during replay.
Preserve original order for kept actions.
solution_approach: Single pass through actions while maintaining deleted entity set.

title: Realtime Presence Aggregator
description: Given join, heartbeat, and leave events, return active users at a query time. A user is active if their latest heartbeat is within the timeout window and they have not left after it.

examples.input: join A@0, heartbeat A@5, query@10 timeout=8

hints: Store each user's latest active timestamp.
Remove users on leave.
solution_approach: Maintain a map of active users to last heartbeat and filter by timeout at query.

title: Service Worker Cache Match
description: Given a cache of URL patterns and request URLs, return which cache entry serves each request. Exact matches beat prefix matches, and longer prefixes beat shorter prefixes.
hints: Sort prefixes by length descending.
A trie can make matching faster.
solution_approach: Use exact map plus prefix trie or sorted prefixes for matching.

title: URL Router Parameters
description: Match request paths against route templates like /users/:id/posts/:postId. Return extracted parameters for the first matching route or null.
constraints: Path segments do not contain slashes
hints: Split routes and paths into segments.
Static segments must match exactly.
solution_approach: Compare segment arrays and collect dynamic parameter names.

title: CORS Policy Checker
description: Given allowed origins, methods, and headers, determine whether each preflight request should pass. Wildcard origins are allowed only when credentials are false.
hints: Credentialed wildcard origin is not valid.
Normalize header names to lowercase.
solution_approach: Check origin, method, and requested headers against normalized policy sets.

title: DOM Event Delegation Path
description: Given a tree and an event target node, return the first ancestor matching a selector predicate. This simulates delegated click handling in a nested UI.
hints: Walk parent pointers from the target.
Stop at the delegation root.
solution_approach: Follow parent links and test each node against the selector predicate.

title: HTTP Cache Freshness Classifier
description: Given response cache headers and request time, decide whether the cached response is fresh, stale-but-revalidatable, or expired. Support max-age and stale-while-revalidate.
hints: Freshness lifetime comes from max-age.
Stale-while-revalidate extends usable stale time.
solution_approach: Parse directives and compare current age to max-age and swr windows.

title: Cookie Scope Resolver
description: Given cookies with domain and path attributes, return cookies sent for a request URL. A cookie matches when its domain and path scopes include the request.
hints: Domain suffix matching is not enough for host-only cookies.
Path must be a prefix boundary.
solution_approach: Check domain rules and path prefix rules for each cookie.

title: Leaderboard Percentile Buckets
description: Given participant scores from a hiring challenge, assign each participant to percentile buckets: top 10, top 25, top 50, or rest. Higher scores rank earlier and ties share the same rank.
hints: Sort scores descending but preserve participant IDs.
Ties should use competition ranking.
solution_approach: Rank sorted scores with tie handling, then map rank to percentile thresholds.

title: Missing Sensor Segment Detector
description: Given timestamped readings expected every k seconds, return missing time ranges. Consecutive missing readings should be merged into one segment.
hints: Compare adjacent timestamps.
A gap of d contains d/k - 1 missing readings.
solution_approach: Scan adjacent readings and emit ranges for gaps larger than k.

title: Feature Drift Alert
description: Compare baseline and current categorical feature distributions. Return categories whose absolute percentage-point change exceeds a threshold.
hints: Normalize counts into probabilities.
Check categories that appear in either distribution.
solution_approach: Build probability maps for both distributions and compare union of keys.

title: Train Validation Splitter
description: Split records into train and validation sets with a deterministic hash of record ID. The same ID must always go to the same split across runs.
hints: Avoid random without a seed.
Hash each ID into a number between 0 and 1.
solution_approach: Use stable hashing and threshold by validation ratio.

title: CSV Schema Inference
description: Infer column types from CSV rows. Types are integer, float, boolean, date, or string. A column should use the narrowest type that fits every non-empty value.
hints: Track possible types for each column.
String is the fallback type.
solution_approach: For each value, eliminate impossible types and return the narrowest remaining type.

title: Experiment Winner Picker
description: Given conversions and visitors for experiment variants, pick a winner only if its conversion rate beats control by at least a minimum lift and has at least the minimum sample size.
hints: Compute conversion rates as decimals.
Apply sample-size guard before lift comparison.
solution_approach: Find eligible variants whose rate minus control rate exceeds threshold, then choose highest rate.

title: Prompt Token Budgeter
description: Select context snippets for an LLM prompt under a token budget. Snippets have relevance scores and token counts. Return selected IDs maximizing relevance with a greedy relevance-per-token strategy.

examples.input: budget=10 snippets=(A score10 tokens5),(B score9 tokens3)

hints: Sort by score divided by tokens.
Skip snippets that no longer fit.
solution_approach: Greedy sort by relevance density and fill the budget.

title: Embedding Nearest Neighbors
description: Given a query vector and document vectors, return the top k documents by cosine similarity. Vectors may contain zeros; skip documents with zero magnitude.
hints: Precompute magnitudes if vectors are reused.
Cosine is dot divided by magnitudes.
solution_approach: Compute cosine scores and maintain top k by sorting or heap.

title: Confusion Matrix Builder
description: Given true labels and predicted labels, build a confusion matrix for all labels in sorted order. Return both the label order and matrix counts.
hints: Collect labels from both true and predicted arrays.
Map each label to an index.
solution_approach: Build label-index map and increment matrix cells for each sample.

title: Batch Inference Scheduler
description: Group inference requests into batches. A batch closes when it reaches max size or the oldest request has waited max latency. Return batches in dispatch order.
hints: Keep a queue for the current batch.
Dispatch before adding if latency would be exceeded.
solution_approach: Simulate batching with a queue and dispatch on size or latency threshold.

title: Label Noise Finder
description: Given model probabilities and human labels, flag samples where the human label probability is below a threshold and another label is strongly preferred.
hints: Check probability assigned to the given label.
Also require a competing label margin.
solution_approach: For each sample, compare labeled probability to threshold and top alternate probability.

title: RAG Citation Coverage
description: Given answer claims and retrieved document spans, mark which claims are supported by at least one span. A claim is supported when all required keywords appear in the same span.
hints: Normalize text before keyword checks.
Inverted indexes can avoid scanning every span.
solution_approach: For each claim, test normalized keyword presence against spans, or build keyword-to-span index.

title: Consistent Hash Ring Moves
description: Given keys assigned to a hash ring and a new server position, count how many keys move to the new server. A key belongs to the first server clockwise from its hash.
constraints: Positions are integers on a ring
hints: Sort server positions.
Only keys in the new server's predecessor interval move.
solution_approach: Find predecessor of new server and count keys in that ring interval.

title: Cache Eviction Simulator
description: Simulate an LRU cache with get and put operations. Return cache hits, misses, and final keys from most recent to least recent.

examples.input: cap=2 put A, put B, get A, put C

hints: Use a hash map plus a doubly linked list conceptually.
In many languages an ordered map works.
solution_approach: Maintain recency ordering and key lookup for O(1) operations.

title: Shard Hotspot Window Analyzer
description: Given request counts per shard over a time window, return shards whose traffic exceeds average traffic by more than a threshold percentage.
hints: Compute average first.
Compare each shard against average * (1 + threshold/100).
solution_approach: Single pass for sum, second pass for hot shards.

title: Circuit Breaker State Machine
description: Simulate a circuit breaker with closed, open, and half-open states. It opens after f consecutive failures, moves half-open after cooldown, closes on success, and reopens on failure.
hints: Track consecutive failures only while closed.
Half-open allows a trial request.
solution_approach: Implement the circuit breaker as an explicit state machine.

title: Replica Freshness Read Router
description: Route read requests to replicas whose replication lag is below the request tolerance. Pick the eligible replica with the lowest current load and update its load.

examples.input: replicas R1 lag5 load2, R2 lag1 load4 tolerance3

hints: Filter by lag first.
A heap helps if lag changes rarely.
solution_approach: For each request, select the minimum-load replica among eligible replicas.

title: SLA Error Budget Counter
description: Given request success/failure counts and an availability target, calculate remaining error budget for the period. Negative budget means the service has exceeded allowed failures.
hints: Allowed failures equals total * (1 - target).
Use consistent units for percentages.
solution_approach: Compute allowed failures and subtract observed failures.

title: Transaction Deadlock Detector
description: Given wait-for relationships between transactions, detect whether a deadlock exists. Each edge A -> B means A waits for B.
hints: Deadlock is a cycle in a directed graph.
Use DFS colors or topological processing.
solution_approach: Detect cycles in the directed wait-for graph.

title: Composite Index Advisor
description: Given query filters and sort fields, suggest a composite index order. Equality filters should come first, then range filters, then sort fields not already included.

examples.input: where user_id=, status=, created_at>, sort created_at

hints: This is a rule-based advisor, not a database engine.
Avoid duplicate fields in the index.
solution_approach: Append equality fields, range fields, and missing sort fields in priority order.

title: SQL Result Joiner
description: Implement an inner join for two tables represented as arrays of objects using a specified key from each table. Return merged rows for matching keys.
hints: Build a map for the smaller table.
There may be multiple rows per key.
solution_approach: Hash rows from one table by join key and combine matches from the other table.

title: Migration Dependency Planner
description: Migrations may depend on earlier migrations. Return a safe execution order or an empty list if dependencies form a cycle.
hints: Same structure as package dependency ordering.
Use indegree counts.
solution_approach: Run topological sort over migration dependency graph.

title: Slow Query Fingerprinter
description: Normalize SQL queries by replacing literal numbers and quoted strings with placeholders, then count occurrences of each fingerprint.
hints: A simple tokenizer is safer than blind replacement.
For this problem, regex-based literal replacement is acceptable.
solution_approach: Normalize literals and whitespace, then count fingerprints in a map.

title: TTL Expiration Scanner
description: Given records with created_at timestamps and TTL seconds, return records expired at a query time. Records without TTL never expire.
hints: Expire when created_at + ttl <= query_time.
Missing TTL means keep.
solution_approach: Scan records and compare expiration time to query time.

title: Password Policy Failure Reporter
description: Validate passwords against configurable rules: minimum length, at least one letter, at least one digit, and not containing the user's email prefix.
hints: Normalize email prefix case before checking.
Return all failed rules, not just the first.
solution_approach: Check each rule independently and collect failures.

title: JWT Expiration Checker
description: Given decoded token claims and current time, decide whether the token is valid. Check exp, nbf, issuer, and audience.
constraints: Do not implement cryptographic verification here
hints: This problem assumes signature verification already happened.
Clock comparisons are the main edge cases.
solution_approach: Validate registered claims against expected issuer, audience, and current time.

title: Access Control Matrix
description: Given users, roles, role permissions, and resource ownership, determine if each access request is allowed. Owners may have extra permissions on their own resources.

examples.input: user editor wants write own doc

hints: Precompute role permission sets.
Check ownership rule after role rules.
solution_approach: Map users to roles, roles to permission sets, and evaluate each request with ownership override.

title: Suspicious Login Detector
description: Flag login events when a user logs in from a new country within one hour of a previous login from a different country. Return flagged event IDs.
hints: Track recent login country and time per user.
Only compare within the time window.
solution_approach: Maintain last login metadata per user and flag impossible-travel-like changes.

title: CSRF Token Store
description: Implement CSRF token validation. Tokens are single-use and expire after a TTL. Support issue(user,time) and validate(user,token,time).
hints: Store token owner and expiry.
Delete token after successful validation.
solution_approach: Use a map from token to owner/expiry and remove on validate.

title: Dependency Vulnerability Gate
description: Given dependency scan findings, decide whether a build should pass. Fail on any critical finding, or on high findings in production dependencies. Dev-only medium findings should warn but pass.
hints: Severity and dependency scope both matter.
Return both pass/fail and reasons.
solution_approach: Scan findings and apply policy rules by severity and scope.
